title: CLI Commands
opening: Complete syntax reference for all `gobbi` CLI commands, organized by command group. For intent-based lookup ("which command do I need?"), see the parent SKILL.md.

content.value: Workflow note management and transcript extraction. Every task gets a note directory; every subagent's output gets collected into it.

rows: Output session metadata as key=value pairs to stdout
Create note directory structure with all step subdirectories and `metadata.json`
Extract subagent delegation prompt and final result from JSONL transcript into `{phase}/subtasks/{n}-{slug}.json`
Extract plan from session transcript and write `plan.json`
Route output to the correct step's `subtasks/` subdirectory. Determines where the extracted JSON file is written.

content.value: Cross-reference: `_note` for note directory structure, subtask JSON format, and when to write at each workflow step.
Manage per-session workflow configuration stored in `gobbi.json`. Sessions are identified by session ID and store workflow state such as base branch, notification preferences, and project name.

rows: Create `gobbi.json` or migrate existing file to current schema version
Read entire session object or a specific field using dot-path notation
Write a field using dot-path notation
List all sessions as tab-separated `id\tcreatedAt` pairs
Run TTL and max-entries cleanup to remove stale sessions

content.value: Cross-reference: `gobbi` skill for session setup questions and configuration keys.

Manage gobbi-docs JSON templates and their Markdown output. The JSON-first workflow: edit `.json` source, run `json2md` to generate `.md`, run `validate` to check schema compliance. Never edit `.md` directly.

rows: Scaffold a new JSON template. Types: `skill`, `agent`, `rule`, `root`, `child`, `gotcha`. Writes to stdout by default; use `--out` for file output.
Convert JSON template to Markdown. Writes the `.md` file alongside the `.json` source.
Migrate existing Markdown to JSON template. Writes `.json` alongside `.md` by default; use `--stdout` for stdout output.
Validate a JSON template against the gobbi-docs schema. Reports schema violations.
Pretty-print JSON template metadata or a specific section.
Show the full gobbi-docs JSON schema specification.
List all JSON templates with type, title, and content count. Formats: `table` (default), `json`.
Show navigation hierarchy as a tree. Formats: `text` (default), `json`.
Search content across templates using regex. Filter by doc type or block type. Formats: `text` (default), `json`.
Extract content by dot-path query (e.g., `title`, `frontmatter.name`, `sections.Setup`). Formats: `json` (default), `md`, `text`.
Run cross-document health checks: orphans, broken links, empty sections, missing parents, bidirectional consistency. Exits with code 1 if errors found. Formats: `text` (default), `json`.

content.value: Cross-reference: `_claude` for the documentation standard, writing guidelines, and block type reference.
Unified health check for `.claude/` documentation. Consolidates reference checking, structural health, schema validation, JSON/MD sync, and completeness scoring into a single pass.

rows: Run unified health check. Default output is human-readable text.
Terraform-plan-style preview of remediations. Shows auto-fixable, suggested, and skipped counts without changing anything. Exits with code 2 if auto-fixes are available.
Auto-apply deterministic fixes (sync-out-of-date, bidirectional-consistency, naming-mismatch), then re-run doctor to show new state.

content.value: Cross-reference: `_doctor` for the maturity model (levels 0-4), check categories, auto-fixable versus suggested categories, and exit codes.
Validate individual documentation files against their expected structure. Unlike `gobbi doctor` which checks the entire corpus, `validate` checks one file at a time.

rows: Lint a documentation file for anti-patterns

content.value: Cross-reference: `_skills` for skill structure requirements, `_agents` for agent definition requirements, `_gotcha` for gotcha entry format.
Send notifications through configured channels (Slack, Telegram, Desktop). The `send` command is for direct use; the event-specific commands are designed for hook payloads.

rows: Send a plain-text message. Reads message body from stdin.
Map NotificationEvent hook payload (stdin) to an attention message
Map StopFailure hook payload (stdin) to an error message
Map Stop hook payload (stdin) to a completion message (with loop guard)
Map SessionStart/SessionEnd hook payload (stdin) to a lifecycle message
Map SubagentStop hook payload (stdin) to a subagent completion message

content.value: Cross-reference: `_notification` for channel configuration, credentials setup in `.claude/.env`, and hook integration.
Session environment setup commands invoked by hooks at session start. These populate `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` with session metadata and project environment variables.

rows: Extract session metadata from stdin JSON and write key=value pairs to `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE`
Load `.claude/.env` file and write exports to `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE`

content.value: Cross-reference: `gobbi` skill for session setup questions and the SessionStart hook configuration.
Image analysis, video frame extraction, and web page capture. Standalone utilities without a dedicated domain skill.

rows: Analyze an image: extract metadata and resize for inspection
Compare multiple images side-by-side via contact sheet
Analyze a video: extract frames and generate contact sheet
Take a screenshot of a web page
Download images from a web page

content.value:
`gobbi audit` is deprecated. It prints a deprecation warning and forwards all subcommands to `gobbi doctor`. Use `gobbi doctor` directly instead.

The former `audit` subcommands (`references`, `conventions`, `commands`) are now internal check categories within doctor's unified report.
